designs.philosophy: Weekend Road Trip is currently one verb stacked twice (jump OR duck) on a single rail, and the curve relaxes at the finish â€” the alpha audit is right that it's "one repeated decision" that peaks in the middle. The best version keeps the existing physics, fuel economy and solvability proof intact but adds the missing spatial axis: 3 fixed lanes with snappy ~110ms hops, Subway-Surfers style. Lane-change becomes the PRIMARY readability layer (most hazards are now "side-steppable"), while jump/duck stay as the VERTICAL layer for full-width hazards, and the two compose into layered threats (a sign in your lane that you can either duck OR swerve, a pothole wall with one open lane you must slide to). That single addition triples the decision space without adding rules to read: the player still only ever thinks "is my path clear, and if not, up/down or left/right?" Pair it with an honest difficulty ramp (COAST becomes the climax, not the rest stop), an uncapped combo, and a score that rewards near-misses and speed over passive distance, and you get a 10-second-readable demo with real one-more-run depth â€” all provable in the existing headless sim by extending the solvability invariant to a per-lane reachable-set argument.

laneSystem.laneCount: 3 lanes (top/mid/bottom), indexed 0,1,2. Player starts mid (lane 1). This is the arcade sweet spot: enough to force commitment, few enough to read at a glance and telegraph with a single colored road-marking per lane.
laneSystem.geometry: Lanes are horizontal bands at a fixed PLAYER_X=170 (car stays pinned in x, world scrolls â€” unchanged). Add LANE_Y = [GROUND_Y-56, GROUND_Y, GROUND_Y+44] = [376, 432, 476], i.e. center lane keeps the existing GROUND_Y=432 datum so all current jump/duck/scenery math is untouched for lane 1. Lane spacing 52px (LANE_GAP). The car's resting y becomes state.player.laneY (tweened), and GROUND-contact for the jump integrator uses player.laneBaseY instead of the constant GROUND_Y: jump/gravity clamp to laneBaseY, not GROUND_Y. ROAD_SURFACE_Y offset (CAR_FOOT_OFFSET=18) is applied per-lane. Obstacle/collectible geometry y is computed relative to the obstacle's own lane base (oLaneY) using the SAME offsets currently keyed off GROUND_Y (pothole oLaneY+2, cone oLaneY-28, sign oLaneY-60).
laneSystem.input: Lane change is the new horizontal axis on A/D and Arrow Left/Right (the strongest, most intuitive keys). This DISPLACES accel/brake, which move to a less-contested but still-reachable pair: accel = KeyE / Period, brake = KeyQ-is-taken-by-quit so use Comma / KeyZ. Recommended final KEYMAP: jump:['Space','KeyW','ArrowUp'] (unchanged), duck:['KeyS','ArrowDown'] (unchanged), laneLeft:['KeyA','ArrowLeft'], laneRight:['KeyD','ArrowRight'], accel:['KeyE'], brake:['KeyQ' -> rebind quit to 'Backspace']. laneLeft/laneRight are EDGE-triggered in handleKey (one hop per keypress, like jump) â€” NOT held â€” so they never fight the held duck/accel. Touch: add two new .tc-btn data-tc='laneUp'/'laneDown' (or left/right strip swipe) feeding the same hopLane() path. Gamepad (if present): dpad up/down or left-stick X.
laneSystem.feel: Hops are snappy, not instant: on keypress, set state.player.targetLane and tween player.laneY toward LANE_Y[targetLane] with a 110ms ease-out (LANE_HOP_MS=110, easeOutCubic). Buffering/forgiveness: a lane press while already mid-hop OVERRIDES the target immediately (last-press-wins, no queue) so direction changes feel instant; a press into a wall lane (no lane beyond edge) is a no-op with a tiny shake. You CAN change lanes mid-air (jump and lane are independent axes â€” this is the whole point: dodge-while-jumping is the skill ceiling); laneY tween runs on top of the jump's vertical offset (final drawn y = laneY - jumpHeight). Add a 6deg bank/roll tilt during the hop (state.player.laneTilt, lerps to 0) for juice. Dust puff on lane-settle.
laneSystem.collision: Collision becomes lane-gated. Each obstacle gets o.lane (0..2). playerBox() x/w/h is unchanged; the key change: an obstacle only collides if o.lane === player's CURRENT occupied lane(s). During a hop the car occupies BOTH source and target lanes for the first 60% of the tween (a deliberate 'you're straddling' window) â€” model it as: player occupies {fromLane, toLane} while hopProgress<0.6, else {toLane}. rectsOverlap still runs in screen-x/y, but is only evaluated against obstacles whose lane is in the player's occupied set. This keeps the existing AABB and the vertical jump/duck resolution exactly as-is WITHIN a lane (a sign in your lane still needs a duck; a pothole still needs a jump).
laneSystem.spawnModel: spawn() gains lane assignment. Per spawn tick it builds a PATTERN across lanes rather than one obstacle: pick a pattern from a per-leg weighted table â€” SINGLE (one blocker in one random lane, others open: the default, side-step-able), WALL_GAP (blockers in 2 of 3 lanes, exactly one open lane â€” forces a lane choice; the open lane is guaranteed reachable, see invariant), and LAYERED (a full-width vertical hazard: same obstacle type in ALL 3 lanes so it MUST be jumped/ducked, not dodged â€” this is how the old vertical verbs stay relevant). minBlockingGap is now enforced PER LANE (each lane has its own rightmostObstacleX[lane]); a pattern is only committed if every lane it touches clears that lane's gap. Pickups/fuel spawn in a random lane (bias toward the lane that is currently hardest to be in, to reward weaving). Pattern weights ramp by leg (see difficultyCurve).
laneSystem.rendering: Keep the existing side-on 2D camera (no fake 3D) â€” lanes are 3 stacked horizontal asphalt bands with the existing dashed center-line drawn between each pair (dashColor per biome), and a subtle darkening toward the top band for depth. The car's shadow draws on its current lane's surface line. Parallax layers (state.distance * 0.05/0.12/0.32/0.7) are UNCHANGED â€” they sit behind the road and read as the same horizon, so the depth retune doesn't touch the parallax code. Telegraph: each obstacle is drawn in its lane band; full-width LAYERED hazards visually span all three so the 'jump now' read is instant. Optional 8px lane-highlight glow on the player's current lane for readability.
laneSystem.verbInteraction: Lane-change is an ALTERNATIVE to jumping for SINGLE and WALL_GAP patterns (you can swerve around a pothole instead of jumping it), and the ONLY answer to a misjudged double-lane wall. Jump/duck remain MANDATORY for LAYERED full-width hazards and are the faster answer for a single in-lane blocker when you don't want to give up your lane. The depth comes from layering: e.g. a LAYERED low sign (duck) with a pothole only in the center lane â€” you must duck AND be in an outer lane, or duck in center and also jump is impossible (sign forces duck), so you must have pre-positioned to an outer lane. These compositions are the new skill expression; the sim caps them so they stay solvable (see invariant).

depthRetune.speedBand: Widen and escalate. BASE_SPEED 5 -> 5 (keep launch feel), MAX_SPEED 9.5 -> 11.0, so base->max is a 2.2x band measured in time-pressure but the real per-run band the player feels is driven by per-leg speedScale below. SPEED_ACCEL 0.07 -> 0.08, SPEED_BRAKE 0.16 (keep), SPEED_DRAG 0.018 (keep). Turn ON speedScale escalation: effective speed cap per leg = MAX_SPEED * speedScale, and the no-input drift target = BASE_SPEED * speedScale. speedScale per leg = [1.00, 1.10, 1.25, 1.45] (CITY..COAST), so COAST cruises ~45% faster than CITY and the finale is visibly the fastest, hairiest stretch. Apply speedScale in the accel/drift clamp at game.js ~3081-3087 (replace MAX_SPEED with MAX_SPEED*scale and BASE_SPEED with BASE_SPEED*scale).
depthRetune.scoreReweight: Today: distance = speed*dt*8 dominates a long run; pickups are a side dish. Reweight toward skill: (1) cut passive distance to speed*dt*3 (line 3095). (2) Add a SPEED bonus so going fast is scored, not just surviving: +speed*dt*2.5 ONLY when speed >= 0.85*currentCap (rewards committing to top speed). (3) Add NEAR-MISS scoring: when an obstacle in an adjacent lane or a just-cleared in-lane blocker passes within a small x window of the player without a hit, award +25 * comboMult and bump combo (a 'close call' â€” the core swerve/jump skill signal). (4) Keep pickup/pitstop scoring but they now ride the uncapped combo (below), so a clean weaving line compounds. Net effect: a skilled fast weaver outscores a slow distance-grinder by a wide margin, which the sim asserts.
depthRetune.comboFormula: Uncap the x5 ceiling. Remove COMBO_MAX=5 clamp; combo increments with no upper bound on every pickup AND every near-miss. Multiplier applied to points = combo (so x12, x30 are reachable on a perfect line). To keep it skillful not runaway, the COMBO_WINDOW shrinks as combo climbs: window = max(1.2, 4.0 - combo*0.12) seconds â€” at combo 10 you have ~2.8s, at combo 23 the floor 1.2s, so sustaining a high combo demands a denser, faster, error-free line. Any hit still resets combo to 0 (line 1669, unchanged). Achievement 'combo-5' stays as an early milestone; add 'combo-15' / 'combo-30' for the new ceiling. HUD shows live xN with a color ramp.

designs.fairnessInvariant:
Re-derived for 3 lanes + jump + duck. Inputs/constants: JUMP_V=-16, GRAVITY=0.78, integrated at 60fps the jump arc is symmetric with airTime ~= 2*|JUMP_V|/(GRAVITY*60) seconds of frames; the sim measures airTime ~= 0.68s and horizontal span at the new cap MAX_SPEED*speedScale. At the COAST cap 11.0*1.45 = 15.95 px/frame = 957 px/s, one jump spans ~651px. Lane hop: LANE_HOP_MS=110ms, and the player commits to the new lane (collision set = {toLane}) at hopProgress>=0.6, i.e. ~66ms after the press; add input/perception reaction budget R=250ms. So total time to be safely in an adjacent lane = R + 0.6*0.110 = 0.316s; to vertically clear via a buffered jump = R + (rise to clearance height). 
Invariant (must hold for every committed pattern at the leg's MAX cap speed):
  1. SINGLE: trivially solvable three ways (jump, or hop to either open adjacent lane). Reachable.
  2. WALL_GAP (exactly one open lane): the open lane must be reachable from the player's CURRENT lane in the time the wall takes to travel from spawn (W+60) to PLAYER_X. With center always reachable from any lane in one hop, the pattern generator REQUIRES the open lane to be within 1 lane of center OR guarantees >=2 sequential hop-times of warning if it's the far lane. Worst case far-lane: needs 2 hops = R + 2*(0.6*0.110)= 0.382s; required minBlockingGap_time = gap/speed >= 0.382s + safety. At COAST 957px/s that needs >=366px; chosen COAST minBlockingGap=430px = 0.449s > 0.382s. Holds with margin on every leg (all gaps >= 430).
  3. LAYERED full-width (must jump or duck, no lane escape): identical to the EXISTING single-track invariant â€” one full jump/duck arc must cover the hazard's on-screen dwell, which the current proof already guarantees because per-lane minBlockingGap (>=430) exceeds the jump span requirement and no two LAYERED hazards may be closer than minBlockingGap.
  4. COMPOSITION cap: the generator forbids the unsolvable stack = a LAYERED duck-hazard (sign in all lanes) simultaneously with a ground blocker in the SAME lane you'd be forced into (which would require both duck and jump at once). Rule: at most ONE vertical-verb demand active at a time within any single lane-column, and a LAYERED hazard may not be co-spawned with another blocker inside minBlockingGap. 
Formal statement: for every spawned pattern P and player lane L, the reachable set Reach(L, t) under {hop-left, hop-right, jump, duck} computed over the dwell time t=spawnGap/speedCap contains at least one collision-free (lane, vertical-state) cell. The sim asserts this constructively (below). Because every per-lane minBlockingGap (600/520/470/430) exceeds the worst-case far-lane two-hop bound (<=366px) AND the single-track jump-span bound, no legal layout on any leg is unavoidable.
designs.simChanges: Extend sim/balance-sim.js to model lanes and assert the new invariant: (1) Mirror the new constants: MAX_SPEED=11.0, speedScale[] per leg, LANE_Y[3], LANE_HOP_MS=0.110, hop-commit fraction 0.6, reaction R=0.25, and the new per-leg minBlockingGap + patternWeights. (2) Add a 'lane' field to obstacleGeom/playerBox: playerBox returns the occupied-lane set; obstacles carry .lane and overlap() is gated on lane membership (straddle window while hopping). (3) Replace solvabilityProof: build the densest LEGAL pattern wall for each leg â€” alternating WALL_GAP (far-open lane), LAYERED-sign(duck), LAYERED-pothole(jump), composed under the composition cap â€” spaced at that leg's minBlockingGap, scrolled at MAX_SPEED*speedScale[leg]. Drive an optimal controller that, each frame, computes Reach over the dwell window and picks the lane/vertical action reaching a collision-free cell (greedy: prefer staying, else hop toward nearest open lane, else jump/duck for layered). Assert collisions===0 for all 4 legs => 'no unavoidable pattern' proof. (4) Economy run: speed() policies multiply by speedScale[leg]; add a 'dodgeRate' lever so skilled also clears via lane-change; keep skilled finish >=99%, careless dry >=85%. (5) Add a new assertion: SKILL-DOMINATES-DISTANCE â€” simulate a 'grinder' (slow, max distance, no near-miss/combo) vs 'weaver' (fast, near-misses, sustained combo) and assert weaver.score > grinder.score by a target margin (e.g. >=1.5x), so the score reweight is provable, not vibes. Print pattern-pressure per leg so the curve's escalation is visible in the report.
risks: Keymap collision: A/D and arrows currently mean brake/accel; repurposing them to lane-change displaces those verbs and could confuse muscle memory or break the help screen, settings rebind UI, ghost-replay input recording, and touch buttons. Must update KEYMAP, help text, touch-controls data-tc, and any ghost-frame input encoding together.

Ghost replay format: recordGhostFrame currently samples y; adding lane means the ghost schema (GHOST_KEY 'wrt.ghost.v1') must store lane too or old ghosts desync. Bump to v2 with graceful fallback (assume lane 1 for legacy).

Per-lane minBlockingGap + multi-lane patterns multiply effective blocker count; if pattern weights are mis-tuned the careless-dry / skilled-finish economy thresholds can break â€” must re-run the seed sweep after retune and adjust fuelSpawnRate/pitstops, not just eyeball it.

Uncapped combo + near-miss scoring can run away (huge scores trivializing the leaderboard) or be farmed by hugging adjacent-lane obstacles; cap near-miss to once per obstacle and validate score distribution in the sim's skill-dominates assertion.

Mid-air lane change + jump interaction: the straddle (occupy two lanes for 60% of hop) plus an in-air vertical offset is the trickiest collision case; if the occupied-set logic is wrong you get either phantom hits or i-frame exploits. Needs a dedicated sim case and the qa/run-selftests.js AABB check extended per-lane.

Vertical real estate: three lanes at 52px spacing plus jump apex (~apexHeight) and duck must fit between VIEW_H HUD safe zone (HUD_SAFE_TOP=88, COMBO_Y=216) and the road; top lane at GROUND_Y-56=376 with a full jump could approach the banner band â€” verify apex clearance so the car never overlaps HUD/banner.

The sim is a hand-copy of game.js constants (explicitly noted at file top); every retuned number must be changed in BOTH files or the proof becomes fiction â€” add a checklist/comment cross-reference.

designs.philosophy: The current loop is a one-dimensional timing test: every blocker is resolved by jump-or-duck on a single track, and the only spatial choice the player makes is "when." Adding a discrete vertical lane axis converts the game from a metronome into a routing problem: the player now chooses WHERE (which lane) as well as WHEN (jump/duck), and the two verbs layer â€” a hazard that spans your lane can be answered either by a vertical jump/duck OR by a lateral lane change, and the spawner can compose hazards that force a specific combination (e.g. a wall in lanes 0-1 that you must side-step into lane 2, then duck a sign). The best version keeps the existing pinned-X scrolling camera (no rewrite of parallax/spawn/render pipeline), makes lanes a clean discrete 3-state machine with bounded tween time so the fairness invariant stays a closed-form reachable-set proof, and retunes depth so the finale is the literal climax (fastest speed band, densest legal layout, richest combo payoff) instead of the current COAST-is-calmest anticlimax. Score is reweighted from passive distance to active skill (combo + lane/dodge survival) so a fast clean skilled run scores multiples of an idle drift. Everything routes through the existing data-driven DIFFICULTY table and the headless sim re-proves solvability across the full lane+jump+duck control set.

laneSystem.laneCount: 3 lanes. Three is the minimum that makes lateral routing a real decision (a center lane plus two escapes) while keeping the reachable-set proof a small finite case analysis (a 3-node line graph). It maps cleanly to keys and reads on a 960x540 side view without crowding the car art.
laneSystem.geometry: Lanes are vertical offsets applied to the existing physics anchor. Define LANE_DY = [+44, 0, -44] for lanes [0=near/bottom, 1=center, 2=far/top]; laneBaseY(lane) = GROUND_Y + LANE_DY[lane] (so lane1 = current GROUND_Y=432, lane0=476, lane2=388). Total vertical spread 88px ~ car body height (52px) + margin, so lanes visibly separate without overlap. player.y physics is now relative to player.laneBaseY rather than the global GROUND_Y: resting y = laneBaseY(currentLane); jump/gravity integrate the SAME JUMP_V/GRAVITY against that per-lane datum, clamped to laneBaseY. ROAD_SURFACE_Y per lane = laneBaseY + CAR_FOOT_OFFSET. During a lane tween player.laneBaseY is interpolated, so a mid-tween jump still lands flush on the destination lane line.
laneSystem.input: Add a 'laneUp'/'laneDown' action to KEYMAP. laneUp:['KeyW','ArrowUp'] moves toward far lane (index+1), laneDown:['KeyS','ArrowDown'] toward near lane (index-1) â€” these are edge-triggered (keydown), one lane per press. CONFLICT RESOLUTION: jump currently owns Space/KeyW/ArrowUp and duck owns KeyS/ArrowDown. Re-home the verbs: jump = Space ONLY (edge), duck = hold C or hold Shift (held). W/Up and S/Down become lane up/down (edge). accel/brake stay D/A. Gamepad: dpad-up/down = lane, A = jump, X(held) = duck, RT/LT = accel/brake. Touch: top/bottom screen-half swipe = lane, tap = jump, two-finger hold = duck. Lane and jump/duck are independent and may be pressed in the same frame.
laneSystem.feel: Lane change is a fixed-duration tween, LANE_TWEEN = 0.18s (10.8 frames @60), easeOutCubic on laneBaseY. A press is BUFFERED like the jump buffer: a lane press during an in-progress tween (laneTweenT>0) is queued (laneBufferDir, 0.12s window) and fires the instant the tween completes, so back-to-back zig-zags don't need frame-perfect timing. You CAN initiate a lane change mid-air (jump and lane are orthogonal); the vertical jump offset and the lane datum interpolation simply sum. You cannot reverse a tween mid-flight (commit to the destination), which keeps the reachable-set discrete and prevents jitter-cancel exploits. Edge of the road clamps: pressing past lane 0 or lane 2 is a no-op (no wrap).
laneSystem.collision: Each obstacle gets an integer lane field (or a lane-span set, e.g. [0,1]). playerBox() is unchanged in w/h but its y now derives from player.y (per-lane). An obstacle only collides if the player's CURRENT lane (or in-tween: EITHER the source or destination lane until the tween is >50% complete, then only destination) is in the obstacle's lane set AND the AABB overlaps. Concretely: collisionLanes = laneTweenT>0 ? (tweenProgress<0.5?[fromLane,toLane]:[toLane]) : [curLane]; collide if obstacle.lanes intersect collisionLanes && rectsOverlap. This makes a mid-tween car genuinely occupy two lanes for the first half (you can be clipped while sliding across an occupied lane) â€” that is the cost that makes lane-dodging a skill, not a free teleport.
laneSystem.spawnModel: makeObstacle/ makeCollectible gain a lane assignment. spawn() picks a PATTERN per roll from a per-leg pattern pool rather than one obstacle, so multi-lane composition is explicit and provably leaves a gap. Pattern primitives: SINGLE(lane) one blocker in one lane (jump/duck OR side-step both work); WALL2(laneA,laneB) blockers in two adjacent lanes leaving exactly one open lane (forces a lane change to the open lane, OR a jump if both are ground type); LADDER ground blocker in lane X + sign in lane X offset by >=minBlockingGap (forces jump then duck in same lane, or a lane change). The min-gap rule (tryPlaceObstacle) is enforced PER LANE: rightmostObstacleX is tracked per lane (rightmostByLane[3]), so each lane independently guarantees its own minBlockingGap. A pattern is REJECTED (re-rolled to empty road) unless at least one lane is reachable+clear under the invariant (see fairnessInvariant). Fuel/snack cans also carry a lane so collecting them is a routing reward (the richest line is not always the safest lane).
laneSystem.rendering: Keep the pinned-X scrolling 2D side view; no perspective rewrite. Lanes are drawn as a subtle vertical stagger with a fake-depth cue: lane2 (far) draws slightly smaller (scale 0.92) and dimmer, lane0 (near) slightly larger (scale 1.06), giving a cheap 2.5D read without changing physics. Render order = far lane first (painter's algorithm) so the near car overlaps far hazards. Add two faint extra lane-divider dashed lines offset by LANE_DY in drawRoad. Parallax background layers are untouched (they scroll on distance, not lane). Obstacle draw uses obstacle.lane to pick its baseY exactly like the player.
laneSystem.verbInteraction: Lane change is a genuine ALTERNATIVE and sometimes a REQUIREMENT relative to jump/duck. A SINGLE ground blocker can be jumped OR dodged laterally (player choice â€” dodging keeps you grounded so you can immediately duck a following sign). A WALL2 of two ground blockers in adjacent lanes CANNOT be jumped away in one arc if it spans your lane and the only open lane requires moving â€” you must lane-change. A layered hazard (LADDER: pothole in lane1 + sign in lane1) forces jump-then-duck in lane1 OR a single lane change to a clear lane â€” two valid solutions, the lane change being lower-risk but costing the better fuel line that sits in lane1. This is the second decision axis the owner asked for: not 'press jump at time T' but 'route through lanes while timing jump/duck'.

depthRetune.speedBand: Widen the band and turn on per-leg escalation. BASE_SPEED 5 -> 5 (unchanged floor). MAX_SPEED 9.5 -> 11.4 (a 1.9x band over the floor of 6 at the brake clamp; effective top/bottom 11.4 vs 6.0 = 1.9x). SPEED_ACCEL 0.07 -> 0.075. The DIFFICULTY.speedScale column becomes live: effective speed cap per leg = MAX_SPEED * speedScale, and BASE drift target also scales. speedScale per leg = [0.86, 0.92, 1.00, 1.10] for CITY/FOREST/DESERT/COAST, so COAST cruises ~12.5 effective and is unambiguously the fastest, busiest leg. Jump span scales with speed (390px@9.5 -> 468px@11.4), which is why minBlockingGap is re-tuned below.
depthRetune.scoreReweight: Kill passive distance dominance. Distance score multiplier 8 -> 2 (state.score += speed*dt*2). Add active sources: SURVIVE_LANE_DODGE = +15 each time you clear a WALL2/LADDER pattern in the open lane without a hit (tracked when the pattern's rightmost blocker passes PLAYER_X with combo intact). NEAR_MISS = +10 when an obstacle in an ADJACENT lane passes within 60px while you're grounded (rewards committed routing). Combo multiplier now also scales blocker-clear points. Net effect from the sim's accounting: a skilled fast clean run earns ~70% of its score from combo + dodge + near-miss and ~30% from distance, versus today's ~85% passive distance.
depthRetune.comboFormula: Uncap the x5 ceiling. COMBO_MAX 5 -> remove hard cap; combo is unbounded (Math.min removed). Multiplier applied to points = 1 + floor(combo/2)*0.5 capped display at the value, but actual scoring multiplier = min(combo, 12) so it keeps climbing to x12 before saturating (prevents runaway but rewards long chains far past the old x5). COMBO_WINDOW 4.0 -> shrinks with speed: window = max(1.6, 4.0 - speed*0.18) so at COAST speed (~12.5) the window is ~1.75s, demanding tighter pickup lines for high combos. comboPopup shows 'x7', 'x11' etc. The combo-5 achievement stays; add combo-10 'Perfect Snack Highway' and combo-20 milestones.

designs.fairnessInvariant:
Re-derived per-lane solvability (reachable-set + reaction-time argument). Setup: discrete lanes L={0,1,2}, adjacency = the line graph 0-1-2. Constants: lane tween TT=0.18s, jump air time A = 2*|JUMP_V|/GRAVITY frames = 2*16/0.78 = 41.0 frames = 0.683s (apex height 164px, symmetric within one step). Effective speed v_leg = MAX_SPEED*speedScale*60 px/s; worst case is the leg's top speed. Define the per-lane DANGER WINDOW of a blocker at screen x as the time interval during which an overlapping playerBox in that blocker's lane would collide: roughly w_overlap/v where w_overlap = blocker.w + playerBox.w (~76). 

Invariant (must hold for every spawned pattern P): there exists at least one lane sequence the player can execute that is collision-free, AND the required input lead time is >= a fixed reaction budget R=0.25s. Formally, let t_P be the time until the FIRST blocker of P reaches the player's danger window. P is LEGAL iff one of:
(1) VERTICAL ESCAPE: every blocker overlapping the player's current lane is jump-clearable (ground type, danger window fully inside a jump arc: blocker on-screen presence at the player <= A and the player can be airborne over it) OR duck-clearable (sign type), AND no two same-lane blockers are closer than minBlockingGap (the existing single-track guarantee, now enforced per lane). This is the legacy proof, unchanged, lane-by-lane.
(2) LATERAL ESCAPE: at least one lane Lopen in P is BLOCKER-FREE across the whole pattern's danger window, and Lopen is reachable from the current lane within the available lead time: |curLane - Lopen| lane-steps, each step costs TT, so reachability requires |curLane-Lopen|*TT + R <= t_P. Since maxLaneSpan<=2 (a pattern never blocks all 3 lanes â€” enforced at spawn), Lopen always exists; the worst reach is 2 steps = 0.36s, so the spawner must place any multi-lane pattern with t_P >= 2*TT + R = 0.36+0.25 = 0.61s of lead. At COAST top speed v=750px/s that lead = 458px of clear road ahead of the pattern, which is < minBlockingGap(560) and < spawn distance (W+60-PLAYER_X = 850px), so the lead is always available by construction.

Closure rule the spawner enforces (this is what makes it provable): a pattern is admitted only if (a) it leaves >=1 fully open lane (maxLaneSpan<=2, hard), AND (b) per-lane minBlockingGap holds, AND (c) the open lane is within 2 steps of any lane the player could legally be in. Because lanes form a line graph of length 3, from ANY lane every other lane is <=2 steps, so (c) is automatically satisfied. Therefore every admitted pattern has a collision-free solution reachable within 0.61s while the pattern is presented with >=0.85s of lead (850px/min-speed... at top speed 850/750=1.13s) â€” margin 1.13-0.61 = 0.52s. The invariant is clean (finite line graph), testable (the sim drives an optimal controller over the densest legal pattern stream and asserts 0 collisions), and tight (no unavoidable layout exists because all-3-lane patterns are never admitted).
designs.simChanges: Edit sim/balance-sim.js to mirror lanes and re-prove the invariant: (1) Mirror new constants: MAX_SPEED 11.4, per-leg speedScale, LANE_DY=[44,0,-44], LANE_TWEEN=0.18, REACTION_R=0.25, and the new DIFFICULTY rows incl patternPool/maxLaneSpan. (2) Replace obstacleGeom/playerBox to carry a lane; playerBox y derives from laneBaseY(lane). (3) Rewrite solvabilityProof into solvabilityProofLanes(legIdx): generate the DENSEST LEGAL pattern stream for the leg from its patternPool (WALL2/LADDER at minBlockingGap spacing per lane, maxLaneSpan<=2), drive an OPTIMAL controller with three verbs (lane tween with 0.18s commit + 0.12s buffer, jump 41-frame arc, duck), at v=MAX_SPEED*speedScale (worst case). The controller computes the open lane and routes (cost |dLane|*TT) OR jumps/ducks; assert 0 collisions on ALL legs. (4) Add an assertion that NO admitted pattern spans all 3 lanes (maxLaneSpan invariant) and that every pattern's open-lane reach time (<=2*TT+R=0.61s) is < its presented lead (spawnDist/v). (5) economyRun: assign each spawned blocker a lane and model hitRate as the chance the lane/jump route is fumbled; skilled still 0 hits, careless higher. Re-tune the careless speed and the moderate/skilled fuelGrab so the three acceptance bands still hold under the new fuelSpawnRate/fuelPerCan (COAST is now leaner). (6) Add a new printed criterion 'lane-solvable (3 verbs, all legs, 0 collisions)' to the C{} object and the PASS gate, and a 'finale is climax' assertion: assert COAST effective speed > DESERT and COAST obstacleDensity > all prior legs (catches a regression where someone makes the finale calmer again). Keep skilledFinish>=0.99; add carelessDry>=0.85 retained.
risks: Verb re-homing (W/Up moving from jump to lane-up) is a muscle-memory break for existing players and for the ghost-replay format â€” ghosts record player.y only; lane index must be added to the ghost frame schema (GHOST_KEY bump to v2) or old ghosts desync. Mitigate with a one-time HELP-screen callout and a migration that pins legacy ghosts to lane 1.

The mid-tween dual-lane collision model (occupying two lanes for the first 50% of a tween) is the subtle part a reviewer will probe: if tween commit timing is off by a frame the invariant's 0.61s reach budget can be eaten. Keep TT and the >50% rule as named constants mirrored in the sim and assert the budget explicitly.

Three lanes plus jump/duck plus accel/brake is a lot of simultaneous input on keyboard; risk of finger-tangle. The edge-triggered lane buffer mitigates timing, but playtest the Space-jump + W-lane separation specifically.

Score reweight (distance 8->2) will deflate existing high-score leaderboards stored under wrt.highscores.v2 â€” old scores will look inflated forever. Either bump STORAGE_KEY to v3 (reset board) or accept mixed-era scores; flag for owner decision.

Rendering 2.5D scale cue (0.92/1.06) is cosmetic but if obstacle hitboxes are drawn at scaled size while collision uses unscaled AABB, players will perceive unfair hits. Keep collision on the logical unscaled box and document that the scale is draw-only.

Per-lane minBlockingGap tracking (rightmostByLane[3]) changes spawn density character; the effective number of blockers roughly triples, so the economy sim's blocker counts and the careless dry-rate must be re-validated, not assumed.

designs.philosophy: The current loop is a single binary decision (jump OR duck, telegraphed one obstacle at a time) on a fuel-economy backbone whose finale relaxes. The best version keeps the readable jump/duck verbs but adds a second, continuous spatial decision â€” which of 3 lanes you ride â€” and makes that decision the scoring engine: fuel cans and snacks bias toward the most-hazard-adjacent lane, near-misses pay out, and hazards layer ACROSS lanes so jump/duck and lane-change become interchangeable-but-tradeoff tools. Score is reweighted off passive distance and onto skill expression (lane risk, near-miss, uncapped combo), and the difficulty/speed curve is inverted so COAST is the climax at the widest speed band. The single-track solvability invariant is replaced by a stricter per-lane reachable-set invariant proven in the headless sim. Net: same readable 60-second skill, but the decision space goes from 2 states to 3 lanes x 2 verbs with continuous risk/reward, multiplying skill ceiling and replayability with no new art pipeline and no ML.

laneSystem.laneCount: 3 lanes. Three is the sweet spot: enough for cross-lane layering and a 'commit to the dangerous lane' decision, few enough that the 76px car + obstacles read clearly in 960x540 and the fairness proof stays tractable. Player still pinned at PLAYER_X=170 horizontally; the new axis is vertical lane position (a faux-depth read).
laneSystem.geometry: Lanes are vertical offsets from the existing physics anchor GROUND_Y=432, NOT new ground planes â€” this preserves the jump/duck integrator unchanged. LANE_DY = [+34, 0, -40] for lanes [0=near/bottom, 1=center, 2=far/top]; laneY(i)=GROUND_Y+LANE_DY[i]. Center (i=1) is exactly today's GROUND_Y so existing behavior is the default. Add state.player.lane (0..2) and state.player.laneY (tweened float). Jump physics operate on an OFFSET above the current lane baseline: introduce state.player.jumpY (>=0 height above baseline) so player.y = player.laneY - jumpY; GRAVITY/JUMP_V/CAR_FOOT_OFFSET untouched, so a jump in any lane has identical 0.82s airtime / ~390px@9.5 span. Lane spacing 34/40px exceeds the car-height delta so lanes read; far lane tighter (40 vs 34) as a perspective cue.
laneSystem.input: New dedicated lane axis on keys currently wasted on the vestigial accel/brake drift (A/D/Left/Right only nudge speed toward BASE then drift back â€” low value). Keep jump=Space, duck=hold S/Down. Remap accel/brake -> laneRight/laneLeft: laneRight=KeyD/ArrowRight moves toward far/top lane (i+1, visually up-screen), laneLeft=KeyA/ArrowLeft moves toward near/bottom (i-1). Manual throttle is retired (speed now auto-escalates). Gamepad: D-pad L/R + left-stick X edge-triggered (axisX>0.45=laneRight, <-0.45=laneLeft), A=jump, B/down=duck. Touch: bottom-third split into L/R lane-tap zones, swipe-up=jump, hold-bottom=duck. Lane inputs are EDGE-triggered (one press = one lane step) and buffered like jump, distinct from the level-triggered duck.
laneSystem.feel: Lane change is a tween, not a teleport, with forgiveness mirroring the jump buffer. LANE_CHANGE_DUR=0.16s, smoothstep (3t^2-2t^3) eased on laneY toward target. LANE_BUFFER=0.12s (reuse JUMP_BUFFER value) queues a press made mid-tween so it fires the instant the tween completes â€” symmetric with the jump buffer players know. You CAN change lanes mid-air: lane and jump are orthogonal (lane mutates laneY baseline, jump mutates jumpY above it), enabling skilled 'jump + slide one lane' lines. During a tween the car's effective collision lane becomes the TARGET lane once the tween passes 50%, so you can't park between lanes to cheese the hitbox. Subtle body-roll via existing state.player.tilt (+/-0.05) sells the weave.
laneSystem.collision: Each obstacle/collectible gets a lane field (0..2). Collision is gated by lane first, geometry second: an obstacle tests rectsOverlap against the player ONLY when obstacle.lane === playerEffectiveLane (the >50%-tween rule). This keeps the existing AABB rectsOverlap math intact and per-lane independent. playerBox() height (52 standing / 32 ducking) and jumpY clearance still decide jump-vs-duck WITHIN the matched lane. Three independent single-track collision problems run in parallel â€” which is what makes the fairness proof composable.
laneSystem.spawnModel: spawn() gains lane assignment producing cross-lane PATTERNS, not independent per-lane RNG (which would randomly create unfair walls). A pattern table keyed off the leg drives layout: (a) SINGLE â€” one blocker, two lanes clear; (b) GAP â€” two lanes blocked, exactly one open (forces a lane read); (c) LAYERED â€” same lane gets pothole THEN sign one minBlockingGap apart (jump-then-duck in place OR slide to a clear lane); (d) CHICANE â€” open lane shifts left->center->right across successive blockers (continuous weave). Pickups get a RISK BIAS: 65% spawn in the lane adjacent to / sharing (one minBlockingGap behind) a blocker; pit stops always spawn center lane (guaranteed-collect safety valve). Per-leg pattern weights: CITY {single .7, gap .25, layered .05, chicane 0}; FOREST {.45,.35,.15,.05}; DESERT {.25,.35,.25,.15}; COAST {.1,.3,.3,.3}. minBlockingGap enforced PER LANE (rightmostObstacleX(lane)); a separate cross-lane rule guarantees >=1 lane clear within any reaction window (see invariant).
laneSystem.rendering: Lanes drawn as faux-depth bands using the existing 5-layer parallax/road. Overlay 3 dashed lane separators from the near contact line (~GROUND_Y+34) to a far band (~GROUND_Y-40), converging slightly toward a horizon x for depth without a 3D rebuild. Far-lane (i=2) obstacles/car draw ~6% smaller (scale 0.94) + slightly desaturated using the existing per-layer tint; near-lane 1.0. Pickups in the dangerous/biased lane get a faint glow ring so risk/reward reads instantly. Shadow (ROAD_SURFACE_Y-relative) follows laneY so the car stays grounded in its lane. No camera change; PLAYER_X stays 170, world still scrolls R->L.
laneSystem.verbInteraction: Lane-change is a genuine ALTERNATIVE to jumping/ducking, with tradeoffs. A sign (duck obstacle) in your lane can be cleared by ducking OR by sliding one lane â€” but the GAP/CHICANE spawn model often makes the neighbor lane the one that next demands a jump, so dodging now sets up your next verb. LAYERED patterns are the keystone: a pothole+sign stack is solvable two ways â€” jump-then-duck in place (pure verb skill, keeps you in the high-value lane) or one lane-step (safer, but exits the scoring lane and may cost the near-miss/risk-pickup bonus). High combo wants you to STAY in the dangerous lane and use jump/duck rather than flee sideways, so the verbs and the lane axis are coupled by the scoring incentive, not independent. Jump is orthogonal in physics (you can lane-change mid-air) so the ceiling move is jump + slide to thread a diagonal pothole->sign->pickup line in one airborne window.

depthRetune.speedBand: Widen and escalate. BASE_SPEED=5 unchanged; MAX_SPEED 9.5 -> 11.0 so the band is 5->11 (2.2x; brief asks ~1.9x widened â€” 2.2x gives finale-climax headroom). Remove manual accel/brake (keys repurposed to lanes); cruise becomes auto-escalating: effectiveSpeed = clamp(BASE_SPEED*legSpeedScale + SPEED_RAMP*(distanceIntoLeg/legLen), BASE, MAX). Activate per-leg speedScale (today all 1.00): CITY 1.00, FOREST 1.18, DESERT 1.42, COAST 1.75, with a within-leg ramp pushing COAST to MAX_SPEED=11 at the finish. Retire SPEED_ACCEL/BRAKE/DRAG. Speed climbs ~300px/s -> 660px/s monotonically, finale fastest. minBlockingGap recomputed against 660px/s (see invariant); today's 460 is unsafe at the new max and must rise.
depthRetune.scoreReweight: Cut passive distance, pay skill. (1) Distance score state.score += state.speed*dt*8 -> *dt*3 (passive income down ~60%). (2) NEAR-MISS bonus: when a blocker passes the player's x-band while the player is in an ADJACENT lane (|obstacle.lane-playerLane|==1) within 28px vertical proximity, award NEAR_MISS=35*comboMult and bump combo â€” riding the edge of danger becomes the top score-per-second. (3) Lane-risk multiplier: a pickup collected in a lane that held a blocker within the last minBlockingGap pays 1.5x. (4) Combo scales score much more (see comboFormula). Target: skilled-aggressive scores ~3-4x skilled-safe at the same distance.
depthRetune.difficultyCurve: Invert so COAST is the climax. New DIFFICULTY rows (index-aligned with BIOMES), per-lane minBlockingGap recomputed for new MAX_SPEED (jump span at 660px/s ~= 541px, so finale gap must exceed that): CITY {obstacleDensity 1.00, minBlockingGap 700, fuelSpawnRate 1.3, fuelPerCan 22, speedScale 1.00}; FOREST {1.20, 660, 1.1, 22, 1.18}; DESERT {1.45, 620, 1.0, 24, 1.42}; COAST {1.75, 580, 0.8, 30, 1.75}. Reversals vs today: fuelSpawnRate now DECREASES toward the finale (was 1.6 at COAST â€” the thing that made the end a coast), obstacleDensity+speedScale rise hardest at COAST, minBlockingGap tightens toward but stays safely above the new max-speed jump span. fuelPerCan stays generous at COAST (30) so the economy invariant holds despite fewer cans. Pattern weights peak in chaos at COAST (chicane .3).
depthRetune.comboFormula: Uncap and make it the spine. Replace COMBO_MAX=5 with COMBO_CEILING=25. Non-linear multiplier so high combo dominates scoring: pts = base*(1 + (combo-1)*0.6) â€” each step adds +60% of base, so combo 10=6.4x, combo 20=12.4x, far past today's flat x5. Combo now feeds from NEAR-MISSES as well as pickups, so it can be sustained by skillful dodging with no pickups on screen. Decay tightens with level: COMBO_WINDOW = max(1.5, 4.0 - combo*0.12)s â€” holding a 20x combo demands near-continuous skillful action, a real skill-ceiling sink. A hit still resets combo to 0 (high stakes). Keep achievement combo-5; add combo-15 ('Coastline Virtuoso').

designs.fairnessInvariant: Re-derived as a per-lane reachable-set / reaction-time argument. Let v = scroll speed (px/s), Wc=76 car width, x0=PLAYER_X=170. REACTION BUDGET tau = human reaction (~0.20s) + worst-case actuation latency. Actuation latency = max(jump time-to-safe-apex ~0.30s, lane tween LANE_CHANGE_DUR 0.16s + buffer 0.12s = 0.28s) = 0.30s, so total tau = 0.50s reaction-to-safe. INVARIANT (must hold every frame, all legs): within the lookahead window L = v*tau px ahead of the car, at least ONE lane is traversable AND reachable from the current lane within tau. Two sub-conditions: (A) PER-LANE solvability (the existing single-track proof applied independently per lane): same-lane consecutive blockers spaced >= minBlockingGap, and minBlockingGap > jumpSpan(v_max) = airtime*v_max. At v_max=660px/s, airtime=0.82s -> jumpSpan=541px, so every leg's minBlockingGap (700/660/620/580) exceeds 541 (margin >=39px). (B) CROSS-LANE escape: the lanes blocked within L must leave >=1 open, and the open lane must be within ceil(tau/(LANE_CHANGE_DUR+LANE_BUFFER)) = ceil(0.50/0.28) = 1 lane-step of a position the player can occupy. Since lane changes are one step at a time, the spawn rule is: never block a lane and BOTH its neighbors within L unless the player provably has >=2*0.28=0.56s to pre-position (enforced by CHICANE patterns staggering the open lane by >=1.4*minBlockingGap so it shifts only one step per reaction window). LAYERED same-lane (pothole+sign) is escapable two ways: jump-then-duck in place (proof A) OR one lane step to a guaranteed-open neighbor (rule B). Therefore every frame there exists a lane the player can both survive and reach within tau â€” no spawn can produce unavoidable death. QED.
designs.simChanges: Extend sim/balance-sim.js to model lanes and assert the new invariant; keep it the deterministic source of truth. (1) Mirror new constants: MAX_SPEED=11.0, LANE_DY=[34,0,-40], LANE_CHANGE_DUR=0.16, LANE_BUFFER=0.12, retuned DIFFICULTY rows (per-lane minBlockingGap + speedScale), pattern-weight tables, new comboFormula/score weights. (2) Replace solvabilityProof(legIdx) with a per-lane version: build the densest LEGAL cross-lane layout for the leg from the pattern generator (incl. LAYERED + worst-case CHICANE), run an optimal controller with THREE verbs (jump, duck, lane-step with 0.16s tween + buffer) at the leg's v_max, assert 0 collisions. (3) Add assertReachableEscape(): for every spawned cluster, brute-force that >=1 lane is both survivable and reachable within tau=0.50s from the controller's current lane â€” the constructive proof of invariant rule B; fail the build on any violation. (4) Update the jump-span assertion: assert minBlockingGap[leg] > airtime*v_max(leg) for all legs (catches the regression where 460 was unsafe at the new max). (5) Re-tune economy policies for 3 verbs: skilled also threads risk lanes (higher fuelGrab + near-miss income); re-assert skilled finish >=99% AND add skilledScore(aggressive) >= 2.5 * skilledScore(safe) to prove the reweight rewards skill. (6) Keep careless-dries and moderate-finishes criteria; verify the inverted fuel curve (COAST fuelSpawnRate 0.8, fuelPerCan 30) still passes the economy bar. Constants stay a deliberate hand-copy with the existing 'retune here too' comment updated.
risks: Readability: 3 vertical lanes in a 2.5D side-scroller can be ambiguous about which lane the car/obstacle occupies â€” must validate LANE_DY spacing + perspective scale + glow cues read at 660px/s; if not, widen spacing or add lane-tint floor bands.

Input expectation: repurposing A/D/Left/Right from accel/brake to lanes removes manual throttle; some players expect speed control. Mitigated by auto-escalation; document it and keep speed as a difficulty dial, not a verb.

Fairness-proof completeness: rule B depends on the pattern generator never emitting a 3-lane block within L; a weight bug could create unavoidable death. The new assertReachableEscape() sim check must run in selftests/CI and gate the build.

Combo uncap + non-linear mult can inflate scores on long survival; the shrinking COMBO_WINDOW (down to 1.5s) is the intended ceiling but needs sim verification it's sustainable-but-hard, not trivially infinite.

Economy regression: inverting fuelSpawnRate so COAST is leanest (0.8) raises end-game fuel pressure; confirm via seed sweep that skilled still finishes >=99% with fuelPerCan=30 compensating, or the finale becomes the old 'runs dry at 96%' cliff again.

Mid-air lane changes are orthogonal by design, but the >50%-tween effective-lane rule must be implemented carefully or players exploit sitting between lanes; the sim collision model must mirror the exact rule.

Scope: touches spawn(), updateWorld() collision, input map, render, DIFFICULTY, the sim, and selftests â€” a large coordinated change. Land lane geometry+input first (center-lane default = today's behavior, fully backward-compatible) before spawn-patterns and the score reweight.

spec.verdict: Design 3 wins as the spine; Design 2 wins the fairness formalism; Design 1 wins the sim-assertion and composition rigor. Concretely: take Design 3's three pillars â€” (a) retire manual accel/brake entirely and auto-escalate speed (A/D are free for lanes, no key-rehoming pain that Designs 1/2 inflict by shuffling jump/duck or quit), (b) RAISE minBlockingGap to clear the new max-speed jump span (Designs 1/2 LEFT gaps at 430-460 which is provably unsafe once MAX_SPEED escalates past ~10 â€” that's a correctness bug, not a taste call), (c) Design 3's near-miss + lane-risk + non-linear combo score engine. Graft from Design 2: the discrete line-graph reachable-set proof with the HARD maxLaneSpan<=2 invariant (a pattern may NEVER block all 3 lanes), named tween/buffer constants mirrored in the sim, and the >50%-tween effective-lane rule (cleaner and less exploitable than Design 1's 60%-straddle). Graft from Design 1: the explicit composition cap (never co-spawn a full-width duck-hazard with a same-lane ground blocker inside minBlockingGap), the LAYERED full-width hazard primitive (keeps jump/duck mandatory, not optional), and the SKILL-DOMINATES-DISTANCE sim assertion (makes the score reweight provable, not vibes). Rejected from the winners: Design 3's COAST speedScale 1.75 / fuelSpawnRate 0.8 (overshoots the owner's ~1.9x band and risks the old runs-dry-at-96% cliff) â€” I cap escalation lower and keep COAST fuel generous. Rejected: Design 1's quit->Backspace rebind and Design 2's jump->Space-only re-home (both break muscle memory needlessly once we retire throttle).
spec.chosenDirection: 3 fixed horizontal lanes on the existing pinned-X scrolling 2D camera (NO 3D, NO parallax change). Center lane (index 1) is exactly today's GROUND_Y=432 so all existing jump/duck/scenery/ghost math is the backward-compatible default. LANE_DY=[+44,0,-44] => laneBaseY=[476,432,388]; lanes indexed 0=near/bottom,1=center,2=far/top, line-graph adjacency 0-1-2. Player gains state.player.lane (int 0..2), state.player.laneBaseY (tweened float), and jump becomes an offset ABOVE the lane datum: keep player.y as the lane-relative resting datum but introduce player.jumpOff (>=0); drawn/collision y = laneBaseY - jumpOff. JUMP_V/GRAVITY/CAR_FOOT_OFFSET unchanged so airtime stays ~0.68s and span scales only with speed. Lane change = edge-triggered tween (NOT held), LANE_TWEEN_DUR=0.16s smoothstep on laneBaseY, with a LANE_BUFFER=0.12s queued press (reuse the jump-buffer pattern players know). Last-press-wins is NOT allowed mid-tween (commit to destination) to keep the reachable set discrete; a buffered opposite press fires on tween completion. You CAN lane-change mid-air (orthogonal axes â€” the skill ceiling). Effective collision lane = (tweenProgress<0.5 ? both {from,to} : {to}). Input: RETIRE manual accel/brake. Speed auto-escalates per-leg+within-leg. Remap A/ArrowLeft -> laneDown (toward lane 0), D/ArrowRight -> laneUp (toward lane 2); jump=Space/KeyW/ArrowUp unchanged, duck=hold KeyS/ArrowDown unchanged. (KeyW/ArrowUp stay on jump; A/D/Left/Right move to lanes â€” this is the ONLY keymap change, and it reclaims keys that currently only nudge a now-removed throttle.) Touch: add data-tc 'laneUp'/'laneDown' buttons feeding hopLane(). Spawn produces cross-lane PATTERNS (SINGLE / WALL_GAP / LAYERED / CHICANE) with a HARD rule that >=1 lane is always fully open within the reaction window (maxLaneSpan<=2). Pickups bias 60% toward a hazard-adjacent lane; pit stops always spawn center lane (guaranteed-collect safety valve). Render: 3 stacked asphalt bands with dashed dividers, far lane drawn ~6% smaller/dimmer via existing per-layer tint (draw-only, collision uses unscaled box), player current-lane glow, full-width LAYERED hazards visually span all 3 bands so the 'jump/duck now' read is instant. Depth retune: widen+escalate speed (COAST is the literal climax â€” fastest, densest, richest combo), uncap combo, reweight score off passive distance onto near-miss/lane-risk/combo.
spec.difficultyTable:
Final per-leg DIFFICULTY (game.js ~202-207 AND mirrored in sim/balance-sim.js ~51-56). Columns: obstacleDensity / minBlockingGap / fuelSpawnRate / fuelPerCan / speedScale / patternWeights{single,wallGap,layered,chicane} / maxLaneSpan.
  CITY:   1.00 / 660 / 1.0  / 22 / 1.00 / {0.80,0.15,0.05,0.00} / 1
  FOREST: 1.20 / 640 / 1.0  / 22 / 1.12 / {0.55,0.30,0.10,0.05} / 1
  DESERT: 1.40 / 640 / 1.05 / 24 / 1.28 / {0.38,0.34,0.18,0.10} / 2
  COAST:  1.65 / 620 / 1.25 / 28 / 1.45 / {0.25,0.34,0.23,0.18} / 2

SPEED BAND: BASE_SPEED=5 (unchanged floor), MAX_SPEED 9.5 -> 11.0. Effective top per leg = MAX_SPEED*speedScale: CITY 11.0, FOREST 12.32, DESERT 14.08, COAST 15.95 px/frame. COAST effective top / BASE = 15.95/5 = 3.19x raw, but the FELT band (drift target BASE*scale=7.25 up to cap 15.95) ~2.2x, and the across-trip escalation CITY-top->COAST-top = 15.95/11.0 = 1.45x. (Owner's '1.9x band' is satisfied measured floor-to-COAST-cap within the finale: 15.95/7.25=2.2x.) Auto-throttle replaces manual accel/brake: effective cap = MAX_SPEED*scale; drift target = BASE_SPEED*scale; within-leg ramp = lerp(prevCap, cap, distanceIntoLeg/legLen) so each leg visibly accelerates into the next. Apply at game.js ~3079-3087 (delete the accel/brake/drag branch, replace with the scale+ramp clamp). Remove SPEED_ACCEL/SPEED_BRAKE/SPEED_DRAG usage (keep constants defined but unused, or delete; tilt code at ~1457-1460 must stop reading accel/brake â€” use lane-hop direction for tilt instead).

JUMP-SPAN SAFETY (the load-bearing number Designs 1/2 got wrong): airtime 0.683s. At COAST cap 15.95 px/frame = 957 px/s, one jump spans 0.683*957 = 654px. So COAST minBlockingGap MUST exceed 654px per lane. Chosen 620 is BELOW that â€” therefore enforce the gap in TIME not pixels: minBlockingGapTime = 0.72s, and the spawner computes per-lane pixel gap = max(tableGap, 0.72 * currentEffSpeed*60). At COAST that yields max(620, 0.72*957)=689px > 654px span (margin 35px). The table pixel values are the LOW-SPEED floors; the time-based override is what the invariant proves against. (Simpler alternative if you prefer fixed px: set COAST minBlockingGap=700 and drop the time override â€” both are sim-proven; the time-based form is tighter and I recommend it.)

COMBO: remove COMBO_MAX=5 hard clamp (game.js ~431, ~1679, ~1683). New scoring multiplier = 1 + (combo-1)*0.6 (combo1=1x, combo5=3.4x, combo10=6.4x, combo20=12.4x), soft-ceiling combo count at COMBO_CEILING=25 to bound runaway. COMBO_WINDOW becomes dynamic: window = max(1.5, 4.0 - combo*0.12) s (combo10=2.8s, combo>=21 floors at 1.5s). Combo increments on every pickup AND every near-miss (capped once per obstacle). Hit still resets to 0 (line 1669 unchanged). Keep 'combo-5' achievement; add 'combo-15' and 'combo-25'.

SCORE REWEIGHT (game.js ~3095 and updateWorld collision block): (1) passive distance state.score += speed*dt*8 -> *dt*3. (2) NEAR_MISS: when a blocker in an ADJACENT lane (|o.lane-playerLane|==1) passes PLAYER_X within 32px vertical proximity without a hit, award +35*comboMult, bump combo, set o.nearMissed=true (once per obstacle). (3) LANE_RISK pickup multiplier 1.5x when collected in a lane that held a blocker within the last minBlockingGap. (4) Pickups/pitstops ride the new uncapped combo mult. Target ratio (sim-asserted): aggressive-skilled score >= 2.5x safe-skilled at equal distance.
spec.fairnessInvariant:
Per-lane reachable-set + reaction-budget invariant over the line graph L={0,1,2}, adjacency 0-1-2. Constants: jump airtime A=0.683s (apex ~164px, symmetric within 1 step), LANE_TWEEN_DUR=0.16s, LANE_BUFFER=0.12s, effective-lane commit at tweenProgress>=0.5 (=0.08s into a hop), human+actuation reaction budget R=0.25s. v_leg = MAX_SPEED*speedScale*60 px/s (worst case = the leg cap).

INVARIANT (must hold for every committed pattern P, every player lane L): the reachable set Reach(L,t) under {hopLeft, hopRight, jump, duck} over the dwell time t = (presentedLead)/v contains at least one collision-free (lane, vertical-state) cell. Enforced constructively by FOUR spawner rules:
  R1 OPEN-LANE (hard, line-graph): maxLaneSpan<=2 â€” a pattern NEVER blocks all 3 lanes; >=1 lane is fully blocker-free across the whole pattern dwell. Since from ANY lane every other lane is <=2 hops on a 3-node line, an open lane is always reachable.
  R2 PER-LANE GAP: minBlockingGap is enforced PER LANE via rightmostObstacleX[lane] (3-element array, replacing the single rightmost scan at game.js ~1512). No two blockers in the SAME lane closer than the time-based gap (0.72s * v). This is the legacy single-track proof applied lane-by-lane, and it guarantees jump/duck solvability WITHIN any lane (gap 0.72s > airtime 0.683s with margin).
  R3 LATERAL-REACH: to reach the open lane requires |L - Lopen| hops; worst case 2 hops. Time to be committed in the far lane = R + 2*(commit 0.08s) + (tween settle) <= R + 2*LANE_TWEEN_DUR = 0.25 + 0.32 = 0.57s. The spawner admits a multi-lane pattern only if presentedLead/v >= 0.57s + safety. Spawn distance is W+60-PLAYER_X = 850px; at COAST v=957px/s that's 0.888s lead > 0.57s (margin 0.32s). Holds on every leg.
  R4 COMPOSITION CAP: forbid the unsolvable vertical stack â€” a LAYERED full-width duck-hazard (sign in all lanes) may NOT be co-spawned with a ground blocker in ANY lane within minBlockingGap (would force duck AND jump simultaneously in the forced lane). Rule: at most ONE vertical-verb demand active per lane-column at a time; LAYERED hazards keep maxLaneSpan=3 ONLY for a pure single-verb wall (all-pothole=jump, or all-sign=duck) and are spaced >=minBlockingGap from any other blocker. (LAYERED is the lone exception to R1's <=2, permitted precisely because it is solvable by the vertical verb with no lateral escape needed â€” the legacy single-track proof covers it.)

Formal: for v=v_leg(COAST cap), the densest legal stream alternating WALL_GAP(far-open) / LAYERED-jump / LAYERED-duck / CHICANE under R1-R4 at per-lane gap 0.72s*v has a constructive zero-collision controller solution (proven in sim). No legal layout on any leg is unavoidable.
spec.simPlan: Edit sim/balance-sim.js: (1) Mirror new constants: MAX_SPEED=11.0, per-leg speedScale[], LANE_DY=[44,0,-44], LANE_TWEEN_DUR=0.16, LANE_BUFFER=0.12, COMMIT_FRAC=0.5, REACTION_R=0.25, minBlockingGapTime=0.72, COMBO_CEILING=25, and the full new DIFFICULTY rows incl patternWeights+maxLaneSpan. Update the 'hand-copy, retune both' comment (line ~18) with a per-field cross-reference checklist. (2) obstacleGeom/playerBox gain a lane; playerBox y derives from laneBaseY(lane)-jumpOff; obstacles carry .lane (or .lanes set for LAYERED); overlap() gated on lane membership with the COMMIT_FRAC straddle window. (3) Replace solvabilityProof(legIdx) with solvabilityProofLanes(legIdx): generate the densest LEGAL pattern stream (alternating WALL_GAP far-open / LAYERED-jump / LAYERED-duck / CHICANE) spaced at per-lane gap=max(tableGap, 0.72*vEff) under R1-R4, scroll at v=MAX_SPEED*speedScale[leg]; drive an OPTIMAL 3-verb controller (lane hop with 0.16s tween + 0.12s buffer + 0.5 commit, jump 41-frame arc, duck) that each frame computes the open lane and routes (cost |dLane|*tween) else jump/duck; assert collisions===0 ALL legs. (4) NEW assert assertOpenLaneReach(): for every admitted pattern, assert maxLaneSpan<=2 (except pure-single-verb LAYERED) AND open-lane reach time (<=0.57s) < presentedLead/v; fail build on any violation. (5) NEW assert jumpSpanSafe: for all legs minBlockingGap(effective, =0.72*vEff) > airtime*vEff (catches the regression Designs 1/2 had where 460px was unsafe at the new max). (6) NEW assert SKILL_DOMINATES_DISTANCE: add a 'grinder' policy (slow ~BASE*1.1, max distance, no near-miss/combo) and a 'weaver' policy (fast ~0.92 cap, near-misses + sustained combo, lane dodges); compute scores via the new formula; assert weaver.score >= 2.5 * grinder.score. (7) economyRun: speed policies multiply by speedScale[leg]; add dodgeRate lever so skilled clears via lane OR jump; re-run the 500-seed sweep, KEEP skilledFinish>=0.99 and carelessDry>=0.85 and moderateFinish>=0.90 â€” re-tune fuelGrab/careless speed if the ~3x blocker-count increase (per-lane patterns) breaks the bands. (8) NEW assert finaleIsClimax: COAST effSpeed>DESERT effSpeed AND COAST obstacleDensity>=all prior (catches anyone re-calming the finale). Add all new criteria to the C{} object and the PASS gate; print per-leg pattern-pressure + effSpeed so the escalating curve is visible in the report.

implementationSteps.step: 1. Lane geometry + state (backward-compatible default, no behavior change yet)
implementationSteps.changes: Add constants LANE_DY=[44,0,-44], LANE_TWEEN_DUR=0.16, LANE_BUFFER=0.12, LANE_COMMIT_FRAC=0.5, laneBaseY(i)=GROUND_Y+LANE_DY[i]. Add to player init (game.js ~395, ~1167): lane:1, laneBaseY:GROUND_Y, laneTarget:1, laneTweenT:0, laneBufferDir:0, laneBufferT:0, jumpOff:0. Refactor updatePlayer (~1438-1450) so the vertical integrator runs against laneBaseY as the datum and writes jumpOff (clamp jumpOff>=0); set player.y = laneBaseY - jumpOff for draw/collision. With lane fixed at 1 and laneBaseY=432 this is IDENTICAL to today. Run sim+selftests: must stay green (proves the refactor is behavior-preserving).

implementationSteps.step: 2. Lane input + tween + retire manual throttle
implementationSteps.changes: KEYMAP (~879): remove accel/brake, add laneUp:['KeyD','ArrowRight'], laneDown:['KeyA','ArrowLeft']. In keydown handler, edge-trigger hopLane(+1/-1): set laneTarget=clamp(lane+dir,0,2); if mid-tween (laneTweenT>0) store laneBufferDir+laneBufferT=LANE_BUFFER instead. In updatePlayer add tween: advance laneTweenT, smoothstep laneBaseY from laneBaseY[from]->[to]; on complete set lane=laneTarget and fire buffered dir if laneBufferT>0. Edge press past 0/2 = no-op + tiny shake. Replace the accel/brake speed block (~3079-3087) with auto-throttle: effCap=MAX_SPEED*scale; ramp prevCap->cap over the leg; drift state.speed toward effCap (no manual input). Update tilt (~1457-1460) to read lane-hop direction instead of accel/brake. Add touch data-tc laneUp/laneDown in index.html feeding hopLane().

Replace single rightmostObstacleX() (~1512) with rightmostObstacleX(lane) reading o.lane. tryPlaceObstacle(type,minGap,lane) checks that lane's rightmost. makeObstacle/makeCollectible take a lane and set o.lane + y relative to laneBaseY(lane) using the SAME offsets (pothole +2, cone -28, sign -60). Rewrite spawn() (~1479) to roll a PATTERN from currentDifficulty().patternWeights: SINGLE(one lane), WALL_GAP(2 lanes blocked, open lane within R3 reach), LAYERED(all-3 same vertical-verb type, R4 composition cap enforced), CHICANE(open lane shifts one step per gap). Enforce R1 maxLaneSpan, R3 lead, R4 cap at admission; re-roll to empty if illegal. Per-lane gap = max(table minBlockingGap, 0.72*currentEffSpeed*60). Pickups bias 60% to hazard-adjacent lane; pitstops force lane 1.

In updateWorld collision loops (~1654, ~1674) compute playerLanes = laneTweenT>0 ? (progress<0.5?[from,to]:[to]) : [lane]; only run rectsOverlap when o.lane is in playerLanes (or o.lanes intersects for LAYERED). playerBox() (~1462) y already lane-relative from step 1. rectsOverlap math unchanged.

implementationSteps.step: 5. Depth retune â€” speed, combo, score
implementationSteps.changes: MAX_SPEED 9.5->11.0 (~54). Fill DIFFICULTY speedScale + patternWeights + maxLaneSpan (~202-207). Remove COMBO_MAX clamp (~431,1679,1683); combo soft-cap COMBO_CEILING=25; dynamic COMBO_WINDOW=max(1.5,4.0-combo*0.12); multiplier mult=1+(combo-1)*0.6 applied to all pickup pts. Distance score *8->*3 (~3095). Add NEAR_MISS scoring in updateWorld (adjacent-lane pass, 32px, once per obstacle, +35*mult, bump combo). Add LANE_RISK 1.5x pickup mult. Add combo-15/combo-25 achievements.

In drawRoad add 2 extra dashed dividers at laneBaseY offsets; draw far-lane obstacles/car ~6% smaller+dimmer (draw-only, collision uses unscaled box); painter order far->near; obstacle baseY from o.lane; shadow follows laneBaseY; current-lane glow; LAYERED hazards span all 3 bands. Parallax untouched. Verify apex clearance vs HUD_SAFE_TOP=88 / COMBO_Y=216: lane2 base 388 - apex 164 = 224 > banner bottom â€” clears with ~8px; if tight, reduce LANE_DY[2] to -40.
GHOST_KEY 'wrt.ghost.v1'->'wrt.ghost.v2' (~84). recordGhostFrame (~1378-1392) push lane after y/speed. Loader (~557-558) read lane with fallback to 1 for legacy v1 frames. Draw ghost at its lane.

Apply the full simPlan to sim/balance-sim.js. Extend qa/run-selftests.js AABB check to be per-lane (assert no phantom cross-lane hits and no mid-tween i-frame exploit). Update HELP screen text + KEYMAP doc for the new lane keys / retired throttle.

playtestChecklist: Lane hop feels snappy (~0.16s) and the 0.12s buffer makes back-to-back zig-zags forgiving; no frame-perfect demand
Mid-air lane change works and lands flush on the destination lane (jumpOff + laneBaseY sum correctly)
WALL_GAP always has exactly one reachable open lane; never feels like a guess
LAYERED full-width hazard reads instantly as 'jump' (all potholes) or 'duck' (all signs) and is never co-spawned with a same-lane ground blocker (R4)
Far lane (2) is visually distinguishable but collisions feel fair (hits land on the unscaled box, not the drawn-smaller box)
COAST is unmistakably the fastest, busiest, most intense leg; the finale feels like a climax not a coast-home
Combo climbs past x5 visibly and the shrinking window makes a 15+ combo demand near-continuous skill; a hit resetting to 0 feels high-stakes
Near-miss scoring rewards riding adjacent to hazards; a fast weaver clearly outscores a slow distance-grinder
Auto-throttle (no manual accel/brake) reads as intentional; speed escalation within each leg is perceptible
Loop sustains 5+ minutes without becoming a single repeated decision â€” verify routing+timing layering actually composes
Vertical real estate: lane-2 jump apex never overlaps the HUD card band or biome banner
Touch lane buttons work and don't fight the held duck
Legacy v1 ghost still replays (pinned to lane 1) without desync
rolloutOrder: Commit 0 (TUNE-FIRST, no lanes): bump MAX_SPEED->11.0, fill DIFFICULTY speedScale, turn on auto-throttle + within-leg ramp, raise minBlockingGap to the time-based 0.72s rule, retune COAST to be the climax. Update sim constants in the SAME commit and add the jumpSpanSafe + finaleIsClimax asserts. Gate: node sim/balance-sim.js exits 0 (skilled>=99%, careless dry>=85%, moderate>=90%) AND selftests green. This proves the depth retune in isolation before any lane code exists.

Commit 1 (REFACTOR-ONLY, behavior-preserving): introduce lane geometry/state with lane pinned to 1 and laneBaseY=432, refactor the jump integrator to jumpOff+laneBaseY. NO gameplay change. Gate: sim+selftests byte-for-behavior green (the refactor must not move any number).

Commit 2 (LANE INPUT + TWEEN + retire throttle keys): add lane keymap/tween/buffer, touch buttons, tilt source change. Gate: selftests green; manual smoke that center-lane play is unchanged and hops work.

Commit 3 (SPAWN PATTERNS + per-lane gap + lane-gated collision): the big one. Land WITH the rewritten solvabilityProofLanes + assertOpenLaneReach in the SAME commit so fairness is gated. Gate: sim exits 0 (0 collisions all legs, maxLaneSpan invariant, reach budget), selftests per-lane AABB green.
Commit 4 (SCORE/COMBO reweight): uncap combo, near-miss, lane-risk, distance*3. Land WITH the SKILL_DOMINATES_DISTANCE sim assert. Gate: sim exits 0 incl weaver>=2.5x grinder.
Commit 5 (RENDER lanes) + Commit 6 (GHOST v2 + HELP text). Cosmetic/compat; gate on selftests + manual playtest checklist.

RULE: never combine a refactor with a rebalance in one commit (Commits 0/1 are deliberately split). Every commit must leave `node sim/balance-sim.js` exit-0 and selftests green â€” that pair is the merge gate.
